group_works.title: Beginnings - Inspiration - the Role of the Writer

results.title: Language - audience
results.brief_description:
Episode number 6 of Series “Writers on Writing”.
Writers on Writing is based on a growing archive of video material, of conversations between contemporary writers discussing their work. The material has been re-edited to form a resource for students and teachers of English, and each of the six twenty-minute videos focuses on different aspects and stages of the writing process. Each video is accompanied by a resource booklet. In this final video of the series, the use of language is investigated, followed by a discussion on the importance of “audience” for the writer.

results.title: Form - Genre
results.brief_description:
Episode number 5 of Series “Writers on Writing”.
Writers on Writing is based on a growing archive of video material, of conversations between contemporary writers discussing their work. The material has been re-edited to form a resource for students and teachers of English, and each of the six twenty-minute videos focuses on different aspects and stages of the writing process. Each video is accompanied by a resource booklet. In this video, a discussion about the difference between writing novels, poems and screenplays is followed by a section on genre, which concentrates on crime writing, science fiction, fairy tales and myths.

results.title: Character - Setting - Plot
results.brief_description:
Episode number 4 of Series “Writers on Writing”.
Writers on Writing is based on a growing archive of video material of contemporary writers discussing their work. The material has been re-edited to form a resource for students and teachers of English, and each of the six twenty-minute videos focuses on different aspects of the writing process. Each video is accompanied by a resource booklet. This video examines how characters are created, and how important are setting and plot.

results.title: Methods - Drafting - Research
results.brief_description:
Episode number 3 of Series “Writers on Writing”.
Writers on Writing is based on a growing archive of video material, of conversations between contemporary writers discussing their work. The material has been re-edited to form a resource for students and teachers of English, and each of the six twenty minute videos focuses on different aspects and stages of the writing process. Each video is accompanied by a resource booklet. In this video, writers talk about how they go about the writing process and the role of drafting and research in their work.

results.title: Writing from Personal Experience - Subjects
results.brief_description:
Episode number 2 of Series “Writers on Writing”.
This series is based on a growing archive of video material, of conversations between contemporary writers discussing their work. The material has been re-edited to form a resource for students and teachers of English, and each of the six twenty minute videos focuses on different aspects and stages of the writing process. Each video is accompanied by a resource booklet. In this video, writers talk about writing from personal experience and in the second half of the video four topics are given as examples of subject matter: children, sex, money and religion.

results.title: Beginnings - Inspiration - the Role of the Writer
results.brief_description:
Episode number 1 of Series “Writers on Writing”.
This series is based on a growing archive of video material of conversations between contemporary writers discussing their work. The material has been re-edited to form a resource for students and teachers of English, and each of the six twenty-minute videos focuses on different aspects and stages of the writing process. Each video is accompanied by a resource booklet. In this video, writers talk about how they began to write, what their inspiration is and their perception of the writer’s role.

group_works.title: Japan - the Zen Way of Building

results.title: Architecture - Quo Vadis?
results.brief_description:
Episode number 10 of Series “Architecture at the Crossroads Series”.
The final part of a series which examines the main themes in world architecture today. The series looks at the work of architects who are at the centre of the current debate, and introduces some younger architects who are beginning to make a name for themselves. Which will be the seminal buildings of the 1980s and 1990s? This final program looks at some of the big projects for Paris, and asks whether the change that architecture is undergoing throughout the world will produce a more humane environment.
